2606.14155.title: Graph-based Target Back-Propagation for Context Adaptation in Multi-LLM Agentic Systems
2606.14155.paper_summary: Context adaptation automates prompt engineering in LLM-based systems by iteratively revising tunable prompts from task feedback, without modifying model weights. Extending this paradigm to multi-LLM agentic systems is crucial: existing methods suffer from inaccurate credit assignment and lack convergence guarantees. We propose \textbf{G}raph-based \textbf{T}arget \textbf{B}ack-\textbf{P}ropagation (GTBP), a context adaptation framework for agentic workflows modeled as directed acyclic graphs. GTBP propagates local target outputs backward through the workflow graph and uses target--output discrepancies to guide a stage-wise prompt update mechanism. Theoretically, we show that GTBP's stage-wise prompt updates become stable over iterations, and that a sufficiently capable LLM optimizer can decrease the overall objective. Empirically, GTBP consistently outperforms strong baselines across three benchmarks while maintaining comparable computational cost.
2606.14155.paper_summary_zh: 上下文適應透過從任務反饋中反覆修訂可調整的提示，自動化了基於LLM的系統中的提示工程，而無需修改模型權重。將這一範式擴展到多LLM代理系統至關重要：現有方法存在不準確的信用分配和缺乏收斂保證的問題。我們提出了\textbf{G}raph-based \textbf{T}arget \textbf{B}ack-\textbf{P}ropagation (GTBP)，這是一個針對建模為有向無環圖的代理工作流程的上下文適應框架。GTBP通過工作流程圖向後傳播局部目標輸出，並利用目標與輸出之間的差異來指導分階段的提示更新機制。在理論上，我們表明GTBP的分階段提示更新在迭代過程中變得穩定，且一個足夠強大的LLM優化器可以降低整體目標。在實證上，GTBP在三個基準測試中始終優於強基線，同時保持可比的計算成本。
2606.14155.author: Tan Zhu et.al.
2606.14155.authors: Tan Zhu, Tong Yao, Kananart Kuwaranancharoen, Amit Singh, Yushang Lai, Deepa Mohan, Shankara Bhargava
